div1.entryFree: Pester, to annoy, to harass, to infest: to be so --ed with a popinjay, H4A I, 3, 50. how the poor world is --ed with such waterflies, Troil. V, 1, 38. who rather had dissentious numbers --ing streets than see our tradesmen singing in their shops, Cor. IV, 6, 7 (== infesting). who shall blame his --ed senses to recoil, Mcb. V, 2, 23. to p. us with message, Hml. I, 2, 22.
